body.notes: Participation in IETF meetings constitutes agreement to the Note Well, which permits recording, transcription, and public archival of proceedings. See also BCP 78 (RFC 5378) and BCP 79 (RFC 8179) for IPR policies.

with communities that that don't really know how to navigate the ietf or other standards processes even uh and and to ideally I would like to see this as a two-way thing where they they bring things to us to register more easily but also in in that process we can give them a little more help in in improving the quality of their registration we we've been dealing out another contexts the idea of making it more efficient and more obvious and better supported to uh to put a tutorial a quasi tutorial or a quite a guide function in the middle of these registration Loops to deal with exactly that problem rather than making the loops both more complicated and easier for stuff to get through because people are unfamiliar with the process I've been thinking recently because of two other contexts that perhaps in the uh in the registration process we need to appoint two new functions one of which is that guide for The Outsider function and the others of which is explicit explicit Devil's Advocate in charge of pushing back but those are just sort of wild ideas okay thanks for the input Mark has taken on action items and we're out of time we have two last calls to write they will run I is we've been asking Atlanta for a long time for some way to have a cue that the reviewers can get access to it's not so much that it's just they have a large backlogger yeah you know
